zh.lifeSummary: Eozen Penteur於0999年出生。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1079年享年80歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Eozen Penteur于0999年出生。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于1079年享年80岁。

nameMeanings.name: Penteur
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: (i) variant of Painter . For example, John Penter in Crowan, 1524 in Cornwall Subsidies , is almost certainly identical with John Paynter in Crowan, 1522 in Cornwall Military Survey . (ii) locative name, rarely, if ever, from one of several places in Cornwall named Pentire, derived from Middle Cornish * pen-tyr ‘headland’ ( pen ‘head, top, end; promontory’, tyr ‘land’). This place-name probably did give rise to an occasional surname, such as that of Thomas Pentyre, 1543 in Cornwall Subsidies , who lived in Pentire, Saint Eval parish. Pronunciation of this surname (with stress on the second syllable as in the place-name) could have been altered to Penter (with stress on the first syllable), influenced perhaps by Penter/Painter in (i), but evidence is lacking. Pentyr(e) in the following examples is usually an alternative spelling of Penter or Painter.

variants.name: Cornwall Subsidies
meanings: English: (i) variant of Painter . For example, John Penter in Crowan, 1524 in Cornwall Subsidies , is almost certainly identical with John Paynter in Crowan, 1522 in Cornwall Military Survey . (ii) locative name, rarely, if ever, from one of several places in Cornwall named Pentire, derived from Middle Cornish * pen-tyr ‘headland’ ( pen ‘head, top, end; promontory’, tyr ‘land’). This place-name probably did give rise to an occasional surname, such as that of Thomas Pentyre, 1543 in Cornwall Subsidies , who lived in Pentire, Saint Eval parish. Pronunciation of this surname (with stress on the second syllable as in the place-name) could have been altered to Penter (with stress on the first syllable), influenced perhaps by Penter/Painter in (i), but evidence is lacking. Pentyr(e) in the following examples is usually an alternative spelling of Penter or Painter.

variants.name: Painter
meanings: English: from Middle English peintour, painter, peintir, penter, pointour (Old French peintour, paintour) ‘painter of images (on walls, screens, shields, etc.), colorist (of statues, figurines, chests, etc.), artist’. In the Middle Ages the walls of both great and minor churches were covered with painted decorations, and Reaney and Wilson note that in 1308 Hugh le Peyntour and Peter the Pavier were employed ‘making and painting the pavement’ at Saint Stephen's Chapel, Westminster. The name is widespread in England.
Americanized form of German   Bender  . Compare   Bainter  .

Germanized or Americanized form of Slovenian Pajntar or Panjtar, topographic names from the field name Pajnta or Na Pajnti, which is derived from Middle High German biunt(e) ‘fenced agricultural land’ (compare German Paintner ), or perhaps from Bavarian Middle High German pant ‘band’ (compare Panter 4).

variants.name: Pointer
meanings: English (southeastern): occupational name, either from Middle English pointere, pointour ‘tiler’ (from an unrecorded Middle English pointer, an agent derivative of pointe, poynte ‘tie, lace, or cord, of twisted yarn, silk, or leather’, for a maker of points for fastening hose and doublet together), or sometimes perhaps from Middle English pointer ‘painter’. Compare Poynter .
In some cases also an American shortened form (or a very rare German variant) of South German (mainly Bavaria) Pointner, itself a variant of Paintner .

variants.name: Pender
meanings: English: occupational name for an official who was responsible for rounding up stray animals and placing them in a pound, from Middle English pinder(e), pender(e), ponder(e), punder(e), puindere, poindere. See Pounder .

variants.name: Cornwall Military Survey
meanings: English: (i) variant of Painter . For example, John Penter in Crowan, 1524 in Cornwall Subsidies , is almost certainly identical with John Paynter in Crowan, 1522 in Cornwall Military Survey . (ii) locative name, rarely, if ever, from one of several places in Cornwall named Pentire, derived from Middle Cornish * pen-tyr ‘headland’ ( pen ‘head, top, end; promontory’, tyr ‘land’). This place-name probably did give rise to an occasional surname, such as that of Thomas Pentyre, 1543 in Cornwall Subsidies , who lived in Pentire, Saint Eval parish. Pronunciation of this surname (with stress on the second syllable as in the place-name) could have been altered to Penter (with stress on the first syllable), influenced perhaps by Penter/Painter in (i), but evidence is lacking. Pentyr(e) in the following examples is usually an alternative spelling of Penter or Painter.

nameMeanings.name: Eozen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Ari, Meyer, Shlomo, Chaya, Elihu, Hersh, Moshe, Shlomit, Tova, Uri, Zeev.
Jewish (Israeli): either an artificial name from Hebrew rozen ‘prince’, or a Hebraicized form of any original Ashkenazic surname beginning with Rosen- or Rozen-.

variants.name: Roznowski
meanings: Polish (Rożnowski): habitational name for someone from any of various places called Rożnów, Rożnowo, or Rożnowa, named with the nickname Rożen (see Rozen ).

variants.name: Rosen
meanings: Ostensibly from the vocabulary word denoting the flower (Latin rosa). However, the name was in use throughout the Middle Ages, long before any of the other girls' names derived from flowers, which are generally of 19th-century origin. In part it may refer to the flower as a symbol of the Virgin Mary, but it seems more likely that it also has a Germanic origin, probably as a short form of various girls' names based on hros ‘horse’ or hrōd ‘fame’. The Latinate form Rohesia is commonly found in documents of the Middle Ages. As well as being a name in its own right, it is currently used as a short form of Rosemary and, less often (because of their different pronunciation), of other names beginning Ros-, such as Rosalind and Rosamund .

en.lifeSummary: Eozen Penteur was born in 0999. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1079, at the age of 80.
